title: Puritan Bennett 980 Ventilator – O₂ Sensor Errors / Low or Unstable FiO₂
description: Troubleshooting guide for O₂ sensor errors, low FiO₂ readings, or unstable oxygen delivery on the Puritan Bennett 980 ventilator using a logical external-first approach.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the ventilator is alarming for low FiO₂ and the patient is connected, immediately assess the patient. Confirm SpO₂ and clinical stability. If oxygen delivery is questionable, transfer the patient to another ventilator or manual resuscitation device with verified O₂ source before continuing troubleshooting. Patient safety always comes first.

steps.title: Verify Oxygen Wall Source
steps.instructions: Confirm the oxygen hose is firmly connected to the wall outlet and ventilator inlet. Check that the wall outlet is active and labeled correctly. Listen for gas flow and confirm normal pipeline pressure (typically ~50 psi in most facilities). Swap to a known-good wall outlet if unsure. Low or unstable pipeline pressure can cause inaccurate FiO₂ readings or O₂ supply alarms.

steps.title: Inspect the High-Pressure O₂ Hose
steps.instructions: Check for kinks, cracks, or loose fittings. Swap the hose with a known-good hose if available. Ensure DISS fittings are tight and not cross-threaded. A leaking or partially restricted hose can cause fluctuating FiO₂ readings.

steps.title: Confirm Air Supply
steps.instructions: Verify compressed air is connected and at proper pressure. Loss of air supply can cause incorrect blending and FiO₂ instability. The ventilator blends air and oxygen. If one source is unstable, FiO₂ readings will be unstable.

steps.title: Check for Active O₂ Sensor Calibration Alerts
steps.instructions: Navigate to the ventilator’s sensor or calibration menu. Determine whether the error is calibration overdue, sensor drift, or sensor failure. If calibration is required, follow on-screen calibration procedure using room air or 100% O₂ as instructed. Ensure gas sources are stable during calibration. O₂ sensors naturally drift over time and require periodic calibration.

steps.title: Inspect the O₂ Sensor Module
steps.instructions: Confirm the sensor is properly seated and connected. Check for moisture contamination or condensation. Inspect for physical damage or corrosion at the contacts. If the sensor is replaceable, install a known-good O₂ sensor and perform calibration after replacement. Do not attempt internal board-level repair.

steps.title: Check for Excessive Moisture in the System
steps.instructions: Inspect water traps and expiratory filters. Look for heavy condensation in tubing near the sensor pathway. Replace saturated filters if needed. Moisture contamination can cause inaccurate O₂ readings.

steps.title: Verify Alarm Behavior
steps.instructions: Document the exact alarm message. Determine whether FiO₂ is reading consistently low or fluctuating. Compare displayed FiO₂ to analyzer readings (if available). Note any unusual noises or gas flow irregularities. Using an external oxygen analyzer (if available) can help confirm whether the issue is sensor-related or actual delivery-related.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming ‘O₂ Sensor Error’ with low FiO₂ reading at 40% despite 60% setting.
CCR.Cause: Found O₂ pipeline hose leaking at DISS fitting. FiO₂ unstable due to fluctuating oxygen pressure.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced high-pressure O₂ hose, verified 50 psi pipeline pressure, recalibrated O₂ sensor. FiO₂ stabilized and passed operational check.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (Returned to Service or Sent to Repair)

title: Puritan Bennett 980 Ventilator – Flow Sensor Errors / Inaccurate Volumes
description: Troubleshooting guide for flow sensor errors and inaccurate volume readings on the Puritan Bennett 980 ventilator, focusing on external causes before internal failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Move the patient to another ventilator if needed. Do not troubleshoot flow sensor issues while actively ventilating a patient unless clinically cleared. Flow measurement errors can directly affect delivered volumes.

steps.title: Verify the Correct Circuit Is Installed
steps.instructions: Confirm adult vs. pediatric circuit matches ventilator configuration. Confirm the circuit type selected in the ventilator settings matches the physical circuit. Incorrect circuit selection can result in inaccurate volume calculations and flow sensor alarms.

steps.title: Inspect the Flow Sensor Placement
steps.instructions: Confirm the proximal flow sensor is installed in the correct orientation. Ensure arrows (if present) align with flow direction. Verify it is securely seated and fully connected. Loose or reversed sensors commonly cause flow sensor errors.

steps.title: Check for Moisture or Condensation
steps.instructions: Inspect the flow sensor for visible moisture. Check the circuit for water accumulation. Drain water traps if present. Replace the flow sensor if contaminated. Moisture alters flow measurement and is one of the most common causes of inaccurate volumes.

steps.title: Inspect the Patient Circuit for Leaks
steps.instructions: Check all connections (wye, humidifier, filters, HME, expiratory limb). Inspect for cracked tubing or loose fittings. Confirm the exhalation valve assembly is properly seated. Leaks can cause volume discrepancies and false flow alarms.

steps.title: Perform a System Check / Calibration
steps.instructions: Remove the patient from the circuit. Perform a full ventilator self-test or extended system check. Ensure calibration completes without error. This allows the ventilator to recalibrate internal flow measurement baselines.

steps.title: Replace the Flow Sensor
steps.instructions: If errors persist, install a new, known-good flow sensor. Re-run system check. Disposable sensors are consumables and can drift or fail over time.

steps.title: Evaluate Alarm Behavior
steps.instructions: Document exact alarm message. Document intermittent vs. constant behavior. Document whether measured tidal volume matches set volume. Document any trending discrepancies. Consistent error after external checks may indicate internal sensing hardware or signal processing issues.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator displaying ‘Flow Sensor Error’ and reported tidal volumes lower than set volume during use in ICU.
CCR.Cause: Moisture observed inside proximal flow sensor. System check initially failed calibration. Sensor replaced and circuit drained.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced proximal flow sensor, drained condensation from circuit, performed full system check. Calibration passed and volume readings returned to normal. Ventilator returned to service.

title: Puritan Bennett 980 Ventilator Fails Pre-Use Check / Circuit Leak Detected
description: Troubleshooting guide for Puritan Bennett 980 ventilator failing pre-use check due to circuit leak detection, focusing on external causes before internal pneumatic failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator is not connected to a patient. Ensure the ventilator is disconnected from the patient before performing any troubleshooting. Leak tests should only be performed with a test lung.

steps.title: Verify Correct Circuit Type Selection
steps.instructions: Confirm that the selected circuit type in the ventilator settings matches the actual circuit installed (adult, pediatric, single limb vs dual limb). A mismatch can cause the ventilator to interpret normal compliance as a leak.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit Assembly from Ventilator to Test Lung
steps.instructions: Check for loose connections at the inspiratory and expiratory ports. Check for partially seated tubing. Check for missing or cracked adapters. Check for improperly installed expiratory filters. Remove and firmly reseat each connection one at a time. Many leaks are caused by incomplete connections.

steps.title: Check the Test Lung and Port Cap
steps.instructions: Ensure the test lung is intact (no tears or loose fittings). Ensure the patient wye is securely attached. Ensure any unused ports are capped. A cracked test lung or open sampling port will trigger a leak failure.

steps.title: Inspect the Humidifier Setup (If Installed)
steps.instructions: If a humidifier is in line, confirm the chamber is properly seated. Check that water chamber seals are intact. Inspect for cracks in the chamber. Ensure temperature probe ports are capped if unused. Temporarily bypass the humidifier with a dry circuit if necessary to isolate the issue.

steps.title: Examine the Expiratory Filter and Valve Area
steps.instructions: Ensure the expiratory filter is installed correctly. Ensure it is not cross-threaded. Ensure it is not damaged. Ensure it is fully tightened. A misaligned filter or debris at the seal can cause leak detection during self-test.

steps.title: Check for Cracked or Worn Tubing
steps.instructions: Run your hand along the tubing and visually inspect for small splits near connectors. Inspect for stress cracks. Inspect for loose swivel joints. Swap with a known-good circuit if available to rule out disposable component failure.

steps.title: Repeat Pre-Use Check After Corrections
steps.instructions: After reseating connections or replacing components, rerun the pre-use check. If the ventilator passes, return it to service.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator failed pre-use check with ‘Circuit Leak Detected’ alarm in ICU prior to patient use.
CCR.Cause: Loose connection identified at expiratory filter housing. Filter not fully seated, allowing leak during self-test.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated expiratory filter, verified tight seal, reran pre-use check. Ventilator passed all tests and returned to service.

helpfulDetails: Whether a known-good circuit was tested
Final device status (Passed pre-use / Sent to repair)

title: Puritan Bennett 840 - Flow Sensor Errors or Calibration Failure
description: Guide for troubleshooting flow sensor errors or calibration failures on Puritan Bennett 840 ventilators.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Move the patient to an alternative ventilator if actively connected. Do not perform troubleshooting while a patient is on the ventilator.

steps.title: Check Power and Connections
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator is properly powered and on a stable AC source or fully charged battery. Verify all circuit connections, including the flow sensor assembly, are fully seated and free from damage.

steps.title: Inspect the Flow Sensor
steps.instructions: Remove and visually inspect the flow sensor for moisture, condensation, or debris. Check for cracks, loose components, or signs of contamination that could impede airflow measurement.

steps.title: Verify Circuit Assembly
steps.instructions: Reattach the flow sensor ensuring proper orientation per manufacturer markings. Inspect patient circuit tubing for kinks, obstructions, or misaligned components that may trigger false errors.

steps.title: Perform Calibration
steps.instructions: Initiate the ventilator’s automatic flow sensor calibration procedure. Ensure the sensor is at room temperature and dry; environmental extremes can prevent proper calibration.

steps.title: Check for Software or Settings Issues
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator software version is current and matches hospital standards. Verify any flow compensation or calibration offsets are correctly configured.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator displays flow sensor error and fails calibration.
CCR.Cause: Observed debris in flow sensor and/or misaligned circuit connections preventing calibration.
CCR.Resolution: Cleaned or replaced flow sensor, reattached circuits properly, recalibrated ventilator. If errors persisted, removed ventilator from service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Flow sensor inspected for moisture or debris.
Circuit tubing checked for kinks or disconnections.
Calibration attempts and environmental conditions recorded.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 - Exhalation Module Replacement and Errors
description: Guide for Clinical Engineering to troubleshoot and replace exhalation module errors on Puritan Bennett 840 ventilators, emphasizing patient safety and stepwise resolution.

steps.title: Verify Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Ensure the ventilator is not actively supporting a patient before troubleshooting. If connected, switch the patient to a backup ventilator.

steps.title: Power and Alarm Status Check
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator is powered on and no general system errors exist. Note any specific exhalation module alarms or codes.

steps.title: Inspect the Exhalation Module
steps.instructions: Check for visible contamination, damage, or obstruction. Ensure module is fully seated and locked in place according to the manufacturer’s guidance.

steps.title: Disconnect and Re-seat Module
steps.instructions: Power down ventilator if safe. Remove the exhalation module carefully and inspect connectors. Reinsert module, ensuring correct alignment and secure connection.

steps.title: Run Ventilator Self-Test/Calibration
steps.instructions: Power on ventilator. Initiate the self-test or calibration sequence. Observe if module errors persist.

steps.title: Check Related Components
steps.instructions: Inspect patient circuit and expiratory limb for kinks or obstructions. Ensure filters are clean and properly seated.

steps.title: Replace Module if Necessary
steps.instructions: If errors persist, install a verified, clean, and compatible exhalation module. Re-run ventilator self-test to confirm resolution.

CCR.Complaint: Exhalation module errors displayed; ventilator alarms.
CCR.Cause: Module misaligned and/or contaminated; self-test failed.
CCR.Resolution: Module removed, cleaned, and re-seated; self-test run and passed. Replaced module when errors persisted; device verified operational.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 - Pre-use Check Failures
description: Guide for troubleshooting Puritan Bennett 840 ventilators that fail pre-use checks, focusing on external causes and safe escalation.

steps.title: Ensure Safe Environment
steps.instructions: Confirm ventilator is powered off and not connected to a patient. Remove any circuit, tubing, or accessories that could interfere with testing. Why: Pre-use checks must be performed without patient attachment to ensure safety.

steps.title: Check AC Power and Battery
steps.instructions: Verify AC power is connected and outlet is functional. Confirm the internal battery has sufficient charge for a pre-use check. Swap outlet or use a verified power strip if unsure. Why: Power instability can cause pre-use check errors.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit and Connectors
steps.instructions: Remove and reattach all patient circuits, sensors, and tubing. Check for visible cracks, kinks, moisture, or contamination in circuits. Ensure sensors (O₂, flow, pressure) are properly seated. Why: Loose or damaged circuits often cause pre-use check failures.

steps.title: Check the Exhalation Module
steps.instructions: Ensure module is properly installed and free from obstructions. Look for dust, moisture, or buildup that could prevent proper calibration. Why: A misaligned or contaminated exhalation module can prevent successful pre-use checks.

steps.title: Run Environmental and Sensor Verification
steps.instructions: Ensure the ventilator is in a room with ambient temperature and humidity within spec. Remove unnecessary magnetic or electrical interference nearby. Why: Extreme environmental conditions can trigger sensor errors during self-test.

steps.title: Power Cycle and Retry Pre-Use Check
steps.instructions: Power off, wait 30 seconds, and power on. Run the pre-use check following standard prompts. Observe the exact error message and which step fails. Why: Power cycling often clears minor electronic faults.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator fails pre-use check; unable to start ventilation.
CCR.Cause: Pre-use check failed due to exhalation module misalignment and sensor connection issues.
CCR.Resolution: Verified power, reconnected circuits, reseated exhalation module, re-ran pre-use check successfully; ventilator cleared for service.

helpfulDetails: Error messages and failed pre-use check steps noted
Final device status (ready or out of service)

title: Puritan Bennett 840 - Internal Battery Degradation
description: Guide to troubleshoot and manage internal battery degradation in Puritan Bennett 840 ventilators, ensuring patient safety and proper escalation.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the ventilator is connected to a verified working AC outlet or hospital-grade power supply. Confirm indicator lights show AC power is present. Why: Some battery faults appear as battery degradation when the device is actually not charging due to external power issues.

steps.title: Check Battery Charge Status
steps.instructions: Navigate to the ventilator's battery status menu. Record current charge percentage and estimated backup time. Why: Confirms whether the battery is holding charge and if degradation is gradual or sudden.

steps.title: Inspect Battery Connection
steps.instructions: Power off the ventilator. Remove and reseat the internal battery, ensuring all connectors are secure. Why: Loose connections can mimic battery degradation symptoms.

steps.title: Perform Battery Test (if available)
steps.instructions: Run the built-in battery test function. Note any warnings or failures reported by the ventilator. Why: Provides manufacturer-recommended evaluation of battery health.

steps.title: Observe Ventilator During AC Power Loss Simulation
steps.instructions: Only if safe: disconnect AC power and ensure backup battery maintains operation for the expected duration. Why: Confirms real-world battery performance.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator battery backup only lasts ~10 minutes and battery warning displayed.
CCR.Cause: Observed internal battery capacity below manufacturer specifications; battery fails internal test and cannot maintain power during AC disconnect simulation.
CCR.Resolution: Ventilator removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for authorized battery replacement.

helpfulDetails: Battery test results and backup duration.
Any error messages displayed during testing.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 - Compressor Failure or Inadequate Pressure
description: Troubleshooting guide for Puritan Bennett 840 ventilators experiencing compressor failure or inadequate pressure delivery.

steps.title: Verify Power and Electrical Connections
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator is plugged into a working, properly grounded outlet. Check the power cord for visible damage. Why: Inadequate voltage can reduce compressor output or prevent startup.

steps.title: Check for Alarms or Error Messages
steps.instructions: Observe the ventilator display for specific pressure, flow, or compressor-related alarms. Document any error codes for reference. Why: This helps determine if the issue is detected by internal monitoring.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit and Patient Tubing
steps.instructions: Ensure all inspiratory and expiratory tubing is properly connected and free of kinks. Check for any leaks at connections, humidifiers, or filters. Why: Leaks or obstructions can mimic compressor failure by preventing adequate pressure delivery.

steps.title: Evaluate Filters and External Components
steps.instructions: Inspect and replace bacterial/HEPA filters if clogged. Verify the oxygen source or air supply line is functional. Why: Restricted airflow can reduce effective pressure output.

steps.title: Run a Manual or Pre-Use Test
steps.instructions: Execute the ventilator’s built-in leak and pressure tests. Observe if the system can reach target pressures without a patient connected. Why: Confirms whether the problem is device-related or patient-circuit-related.

steps.title: Listen and Observe Compressor Operation
steps.instructions: With the ventilator on, listen for unusual sounds (straining, failure to start, or irregular tone). Observe pressure waveforms on the display. Why: Audible or visual cues can help identify mechanical failure.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator failing to achieve set inspiratory pressures; alarms triggered.
CCR.Cause: External inspection revealed no tubing leaks; filters clear; compressor produces abnormal noise during test.
CCR.Resolution: Ventilator removed from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for manufacturer repair.

helpfulDetails: Final device status: Out of Service

title: Puritan Bennett 560 - Turbine Failure or Insufficient Flow
description: This guide assists Clinical Engineering in troubleshooting Puritan Bennett 560 ventilators experiencing turbine failure or insufficient airflow.

steps.title: Check Power Supply
steps.instructions: Verify the ventilator is properly plugged into a verified working outlet. Confirm the power switch is ON and the unit completes its startup sequence. Why: Turbine motors rely on consistent power; low or interrupted power can reduce airflow.

steps.title: Inspect Air Intake and Filters
steps.instructions: Remove and inspect the air intake filter for blockage or excessive dust. Clean or replace filters as necessary. Why: Restricted airflow to the turbine can reduce delivered flow and trigger low-flow alarms.

steps.title: Check Circuit and Tubing Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure patient circuit, inspiratory, and expiratory tubing are correctly connected and not kinked. Verify that humidifiers or inline filters are not clogged. Why: Mechanical restrictions in the breathing circuit can mimic turbine failure.

steps.title: Observe Turbine Operation
steps.instructions: Listen for abnormal turbine sounds (grinding, excessive vibration). Ensure the turbine spins freely when running a self-test. Why: Noise or vibration can indicate mechanical wear or impending turbine failure.

steps.title: Run Ventilator Self-Test or Diagnostic Mode
steps.instructions: Follow the ventilator prompts to perform a full self-test. Document any flow or turbine-related errors. Why: The self-test can confirm whether the turbine motor or sensors are functioning properly.

steps.title: Check Environmental Conditions
steps.instructions: Ensure ambient temperature is within the operating range. Remove any dust or obstruction around the ventilator’s air intake. Why: Overheating or restricted airflow around the unit can reduce turbine efficiency.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator delivering insufficient flow; alarms triggered; turbine suspected.
CCR.Cause: Air intake filter partially blocked; tubing unobstructed; turbine self-test indicates error code 12 (turbine malfunction).
CCR.Resolution: Replaced air filter and ran diagnostic; issue persists. Device labeled Out of Service and sent for repair.

helpfulDetails: Tubing and circuit checked for obstructions
Self-test results and error codes documented
Turbine noise or unusual vibration observed

title: Puritan Bennett 560 - Battery Not Charging or Shortened Runtime
description: Guide for troubleshooting battery issues on Puritan Bennett 560 ventilators, including failure to charge and shortened runtime.

steps.title: Verify AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the ventilator is connected to a working outlet. Test the outlet with a known working device. Why: A non-functional outlet or loose connection will prevent charging.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions: Check for frayed cables, broken connectors, or loose plugs. Ensure the cord is fully seated in both the ventilator and outlet. Why: Damaged or loose cords prevent proper power delivery.

steps.title: Check Battery Seating
steps.instructions: Remove the battery and reseat it according to manufacturer instructions. Ensure locking mechanism engages fully. Why: Improperly seated batteries may not charge or provide full runtime.

steps.title: Observe Ventilator Indicators
steps.instructions: Check charging LED or screen battery icon for status. Note any error messages related to battery or power. Why: Indicators can reveal whether the device recognizes the battery.

steps.title: Test with Spare/Service Battery
steps.instructions: If available, replace the battery with a known good unit. Observe charging behavior and runtime. Why: Confirms if the original battery is degraded or defective.

steps.title: Allow Full Charge Cycle
steps.instructions: Connect the battery to AC power for the recommended charging time. Monitor if the battery reaches full charge and maintains it. Why: Short or interrupted charge cycles may give the appearance of failure.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator battery not charging and provides only 10 minutes of runtime.
CCR.Cause: Battery seated correctly but fails to accept charge; LED indicates low battery despite AC power.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced battery with known good unit; device charges and holds runtime as expected. Original battery labeled for replacement/repair.

title: Puritan Bennett 560 - Circuit Disconnect Alarms
description: Guide for troubleshooting circuit disconnect alarms on Puritan Bennett 560 ventilators, focusing on external causes before internal faults.

steps.title: Verify Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Ensure the patient is stable and ventilated by another device or manual ventilation if needed. Never troubleshoot an active patient on the affected ventilator.

steps.title: Check Circuit Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect all tubing connections from the ventilator to the patient. Ensure inspiratory and expiratory limbs are properly seated. Confirm humidifier, filters, and adapters are correctly installed. Loose or misaligned connections are the most common cause of disconnect alarms.

steps.title: Inspect for Leaks or Damage
steps.instructions: Check the circuit for cracks, holes, or kinks. Ensure all ports on the ventilator and patient interface are sealed. Replace any damaged tubing or connectors.

steps.title: Verify Flow Sensors and Adapters
steps.instructions: Inspect the flow sensor, if used, for proper placement and cleanliness. Make sure any D-lite or patient adapters are seated correctly.

steps.title: Check Ventilator Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator mode matches the circuit configuration (e.g., single limb vs. dual limb). Ensure tidal volume and alarms are appropriately set.

steps.title: Power Cycle the Ventilator
steps.instructions: Turn the ventilator off and back on to clear false alarms after verifying the circuit is intact.

steps.title: Observe Alarm Behavior
steps.instructions: If alarms persist, note whether they occur immediately upon connection or intermittently during operation. Document any unusual sounds, leaks, or error codes.

CCR.Complaint: User reports repeated “circuit disconnect” alarms while ventilating patient.
CCR.Cause: Loose expiratory limb connection and kinked inspiratory tubing observed during inspection.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected tubing securely, replaced damaged sections, verified circuit integrity, ventilator returned to service.

helpfulDetails: No unusual sounds, heat, or odor detected

title: Puritan Bennett 560 - Pre-use Self-test Failures
description: Guide to troubleshoot Puritan Bennett 560 ventilators that fail pre-use self-test, focusing on external factors before internal failure.

steps.title: Verify Power Source
steps.instructions: Ensure the ventilator is connected to a functional AC outlet. Check that the power cord is fully seated. Why: Inadequate power can cause self-test failure or incomplete initialization.

steps.title: Check Battery Status
steps.instructions: Confirm the internal battery has sufficient charge. If battery is low, allow charging or replace with a known good battery. Why: Low battery can prevent successful self-test completion.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit and Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure patient circuit is correctly attached, no kinks, leaks, or loose connections. Verify sensors, flow sensors, and pressure lines are properly seated. Why: Disconnects or leaks often trigger self-test failures.

steps.title: Confirm Environmental Conditions
steps.instructions: Ensure ambient temperature and humidity are within manufacturer specifications. Remove any obstructions near air intakes or exhausts. Why: Extreme conditions can affect turbine performance and self-test.

steps.title: Reset the Ventilator
steps.instructions: Power down, wait 30 seconds, and power back on. Run the self-test again. Why: Sometimes a simple reset resolves minor electronic glitches.

steps.title: Swap Known Good Accessories
steps.instructions: Replace patient circuit, flow sensors, or connectors with verified functional units. Why: External components can cause false test failures.

steps.title: Check for Active Alarms or Error Logs
steps.instructions: Review any messages displayed during self-test. Document alarm codes for reference or escalation. Why: Error codes can indicate specific sensors or components causing failure.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator fails pre-use self-test; displays error codes and will not enter clinical service.
CCR.Cause: External verification showed patient circuit disconnected and low battery. All accessories reseated, power verified, environmental conditions normal.
CCR.Resolution: Ventilator reset and self-test rerun after reconnections. If failure persists, removed from service and labeled Out of Service for repair.

helpfulDetails: Final device status (operational or Out of Service)

title: Puritan Bennett 560 - Oxygen Sensor Errors
description: Guide for troubleshooting oxygen sensor errors on PB560 ventilators, ensuring accurate FiO₂ delivery and patient safety.

steps.title: Verify power and device readiness
steps.instructions: Ensure the ventilator is powered on and has passed its basic self-test. Confirm that any pre-use check screens are clear before proceeding. Why it matters: Sensor errors can sometimes be falsely reported if the device is in a transitional state or not fully initialized.

steps.title: Inspect the oxygen sensor physically
steps.instructions: Locate the oxygen sensor (typically near the inspiratory limb or inside the sensor module). Check for obvious damage, contamination, or moisture on the sensor tip. Ensure it is securely seated in its connector. Why it matters: Loose or contaminated sensors are the most common cause of errors.

steps.title: Check connections and wiring
steps.instructions: Verify that the sensor cable is not frayed, bent, or pinched. Disconnect and reconnect the sensor to confirm a solid connection. Why it matters: Poor connections can cause intermittent readings or trigger error codes.

steps.title: Review sensor calibration status
steps.instructions: Check the ventilator menu for sensor calibration status. If the sensor requires calibration or replacement, note the date of last calibration. Why it matters: Some sensors have a limited life and must be recalibrated to function properly.

steps.title: Swap with a known good sensor (if available)
steps.instructions: Replace the current sensor with a functioning spare. Observe if the error persists. Why it matters: Confirms whether the issue is with the sensor itself or the ventilator electronics.

steps.title: Verify FiO₂ delivery and readings
steps.instructions: Run a test on the ventilator with the patient circuit disconnected (ensure patient safety). Observe the oxygen concentration readings for stability and accuracy. Why it matters: Helps ensure the ventilator reports accurate oxygen levels before patient use.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator shows oxygen sensor error during pre-use check.
CCR.Cause: Sensor cable was loose, or sensor tip contaminated (observed during inspection).
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected the sensor securely and cleaned the sensor tip. Tested ventilator; error resolved. If unresolved, note sensor replacement or device sent to service.

helpfulDetails: Sensor serial number and installation date

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: AC POWER LOSS Operating on battery
description: Troubleshooting guide for the AC POWER LOSS Operating on battery alarm on the Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator, indicating loss of AC input and risk of shutdown if external power is not restored.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Immediately confirm the patient is being ventilated adequately. Prepare backup ventilation such as a manual resuscitator or alternate ventilator if needed. Do not allow troubleshooting to interrupt patient support.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the AC POWER LOSS alarm is active. Check that the ventilator indicates battery operation, the AC power indicator is off, the audible alarm is present, and any battery runtime countdown is displayed.

steps.title: Check AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Verify the outlet supplying the ventilator is active using a known-good device. Confirm connection to the correct hospital-grade or emergency power outlet if applicable.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord and Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure the AC power cord is firmly seated at both the wall outlet and the ventilator. Inspect for cuts, fraying, or loose connectors, then reseat the cord securely.

steps.title: Check Power Strip or Extension Use
steps.instructions: If the ventilator is connected through a power strip or extension, bypass it and plug directly into the wall outlet. Verify any intermediate device is powered on and functioning.

steps.title: Verify Facility Power Availability
steps.instructions: Check nearby equipment for power, confirm whether the outlet is on emergency power, and ask staff if there was a recent localized or broader power interruption.

steps.title: Observe for AC Power Recovery
steps.instructions: After correcting the external power source, confirm the AC power indicator turns on, verify the ventilator returns to AC power, and ensure the alarm clears automatically.

steps.title: Remove from Service if Alarm Persists
steps.instructions: If AC power is confirmed at the outlet and all external causes have been ruled out but the ventilator remains on battery, remove the device from service, label it Out of Service, and send it for repair or bench evaluation.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarmed AC POWER LOSS and switched to battery operation during patient use.
CCR.Cause: AC power source was unavailable or the power cord connection at the wall outlet was loose, preventing AC input to the ventilator.
CCR.Resolution: Reseated the power cord, confirmed outlet functionality, verified return to AC power, and confirmed the alarm cleared. Device returned to service after normal operation was verified.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: LOW AC POWER mains power below acceptable level
description: LOW AC POWER alarm indicating insufficient mains voltage leading to battery use, unstable operation, or risk of ventilator shutdown

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Transfer patient to backup ventilator if needed and confirm continuous ventilation.

steps.title: Verify Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm alarm presence, observe power indicators, and check for battery switching.

steps.title: Check AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Test outlet with known-good device and try alternate verified outlet.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord
steps.instructions: Ensure secure connections and check for damage or wear.

steps.title: Check Power Distribution
steps.instructions: Remove extension cords and plug directly into wall outlet.

steps.title: Verify Voltage Configuration
steps.instructions: Confirm correct voltage setting in service mode.

steps.title: Observe for Resolution
steps.instructions: Confirm alarm clears and unit remains on stable AC power.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming LOW AC POWER; unit intermittently switching to battery during use
CCR.Cause: Verified stable wall power and connections; alarm persisted indicating likely internal power supply issue
CCR.Resolution: Removed ventilator from service and sent for repair evaluation

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: LOSS OF POWER insufficient AC and battery power
description: LOSS OF POWER alarm indicating insufficient AC and battery power leading to ventilator shutdown or failure to operate.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove ventilator from use and transition patient to backup ventilation or manual support.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm LOSS OF POWER alarm and observe power behavior and timing of failure.

steps.title: Check AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Verify outlet functionality using a known-good device and proper connection.

steps.title: Inspect Power Cord
steps.instructions: Check for secure connection and damage; replace if necessary.

steps.title: Verify Battery Condition
steps.instructions: Test battery backup by disconnecting AC and observing runtime.

steps.title: Perform Power Reset
steps.instructions: Cycle power after confirming proper AC connection.

steps.title: Isolate Cause
steps.instructions: Determine if issue is outlet, cord, battery, or internal power supply.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator displaying LOSS OF POWER alarm and not powering on.
CCR.Cause: Battery failure or internal power system suspected after external power verified.
CCR.Resolution: Verified outlet and cord; removed device from service and sent for repair.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: LOW BATTERY Battery has < 2 minutes remaining
description: LOW BATTERY alarm indicating less than 2 minutes of power remaining, risking imminent ventilator shutdown.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm ventilation, prepare BVM, and transfer patient to backup ventilator if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm LOW BATTERY alarm, low battery indicator, and operation without AC power.

steps.title: Check AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Verify outlet functionality, plug connection, and hospital-grade power source.

steps.title: Check Power Cord
steps.instructions: Inspect and reseat power cord; check for damage.

steps.title: Check Power Distribution
steps.instructions: Inspect power strips, towers, and breakers; try alternate outlet.

steps.title: Confirm Battery Charging
steps.instructions: Verify battery begins charging once AC power is restored.

steps.title: Stop if Resolved
steps.instructions: Return to service and monitor charging status.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming LOW BATTERY with less than 2 minutes remaining.
CCR.Cause: Loss of AC power or internal battery/charging failure.
CCR.Resolution: Restored AC power and confirmed charging or removed from service for repair.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: INOPERATIVE BATTERY Battery power source not functioning
description: Troubleshooting guide for the INOPERATIVE BATTERY alarm indicating loss of battery backup and risk of ventilator shutdown during AC power failure.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: If the ventilator is in use, confirm the patient is being ventilated safely, connect to confirmed AC power, and move the patient to a backup ventilator or alternate ventilation method if backup power is uncertain.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Power on the ventilator, confirm the INOPERATIVE BATTERY alarm, and observe whether battery runtime is unavailable or battery status indicates failure.

steps.title: Check the AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Verify the ventilator is plugged into a known-good AC outlet, test the outlet if needed, and confirm the unit operates normally on AC power.

steps.title: Inspect the Power Cord and AC Distribution Path
steps.instructions: Inspect the power cord and all AC input connections for damage, looseness, or wear, and reseat connections to confirm stable AC input.

steps.title: Perform a Power Cycle
steps.instructions: Turn the ventilator off, wait 30 to 60 seconds, power it back on, and check whether the alarm clears.

steps.title: Run the Extended Self Test (EST)
steps.instructions: Run the EST according to procedure and review whether it identifies a battery or internal power-system fault.

steps.title: Check Battery Function Only When Safe
steps.instructions: When the ventilator is off patient, briefly disconnect AC power and observe whether the ventilator remains powered or shuts down immediately.

steps.title: Remove from Service if Alarm Persists
steps.instructions: If the alarm remains after external checks and EST, remove the device from service, label it Out of Service, and send it for repair or battery replacement.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator displayed INOPERATIVE BATTERY alarm and would not maintain power when unplugged from AC.
CCR.Cause: Verified stable AC power input. EST indicated battery failure, and the ventilator shut down immediately when AC power was briefly removed off patient.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ventilator from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for battery replacement and bench evaluation.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: DEVICE ALERT POST or background diagnostic failure
description: DEVICE ALERT alarm indicating POST or background diagnostic failure leading to startup issues or unreliable ventilator operation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Remove ventilator from use and transition patient to backup device.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm DEVICE ALERT presence and behavior during startup or operation.

steps.title: Power Cycle Device
steps.instructions: Turn off, disconnect power, wait, and restart ventilator.

steps.title: Check AC Power Source
steps.instructions: Verify stable outlet, inspect cord and connections.

steps.title: Inspect External Components
steps.instructions: Check cables, modules, and accessories for issues.

steps.title: Review Diagnostic Logs
steps.instructions: Identify repeated faults or subsystem failures.

steps.title: Run POST / EST
steps.instructions: Restart and run diagnostics to confirm system integrity.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator displaying DEVICE ALERT alarm during startup or operation.
CCR.Cause: POST or background diagnostic failure indicating internal system fault.
CCR.Resolution: Performed power cycle, verified power, checked connections, reviewed logs, reran diagnostics. Alarm persisted; unit removed from service.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: APNEA No breath detected within apnea interval
description: APNEA alarm indicating no breaths detected within the apnea interval, risking loss of ventilation and patient respiratory compromise.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient immediately, initiate manual ventilation if needed, and transition to backup ventilator.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm APNEA alarm is present and ventilator is not detecting breaths.

steps.title: Check Patient Condition
steps.instructions: Assess chest rise, airway patency, and monitor readings to confirm true apnea.

steps.title: Check Settings
steps.instructions: Verify apnea interval, backup ventilation, and trigger sensitivity settings.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit
steps.instructions: Check for leaks, disconnections, and secure airway connections.

steps.title: Check Sensors
steps.instructions: Inspect and replace flow sensor if contaminated or faulty.

steps.title: Reset System
steps.instructions: If stable, power cycle ventilator after placing patient on alternate support.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming APNEA; no breaths detected.
CCR.Cause: Alarm persisted after external checks; possible internal sensing failure or true apnea.
CCR.Resolution: Removed from service, placed patient on backup ventilator, sent unit for evaluation.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: CIRCUIT DISCONNECT No ventilation. Check patient/ventilator status.
description: CIRCUIT DISCONNECT alarm indicating loss of ventilation due to disconnected circuit or airflow interruption, requiring immediate patient safety intervention.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient immediately and provide manual ventilation or switch to a backup ventilator.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm CIRCUIT DISCONNECT alarm is active and no breaths are being delivered.

steps.title: Check Circuit Connections
steps.instructions: Inspect and reseat all ventilator-to-patient circuit connections.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit Integrity
steps.instructions: Check for leaks, cracks, or disconnections and replace circuit if needed.

steps.title: Check for Power Recovery State
steps.instructions: Verify if recent power loss caused ventilator to enter idle or disconnect condition.

steps.title: Run EST
steps.instructions: Remove from patient use and perform Extended Self-Test to verify system function.

steps.title: Swap Circuit
steps.instructions: Replace with a known-good circuit to isolate accessory-related faults.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming CIRCUIT DISCONNECT with no ventilation delivered.
CCR.Cause: Loose circuit connection or possible internal sensing issue.
CCR.Resolution: Re-secured connections or removed device from service for repair after failed testing.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: SEVERE OCCLUSION Severe airway obstruction or blocked circuit
description: SEVERE OCCLUSION alarm indicating blocked airflow and inability to ventilate the patient effectively.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient immediately and provide alternate ventilation if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm SEVERE OCCLUSION alarm and observe pressure and volume behavior.

steps.title: Check Patient
steps.instructions: Inspect airway for obstruction, kinks, or secretions.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit
steps.instructions: Check tubing for kinks, blockage, or condensation.

steps.title: Check Filters
steps.instructions: Inspect and replace clogged filters or HMEs.

steps.title: Verify Exhalation Path
steps.instructions: Ensure expiratory limb and valve are clear.

steps.title: Isolate Issue
steps.instructions: Test with a test lung to determine external vs internal cause.

steps.title: Run EST
steps.instructions: Perform extended self-test to verify ventilator function.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming SEVERE OCCLUSION with no delivered tidal volume.
CCR.Cause: Circuit obstruction due to condensation or airway blockage (or internal fault if unresolved).
CCR.Resolution: Cleared obstruction and verified function with EST or removed device from service.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: LOW INSP PRESSURE Inspiratory pressure below expected target
description: LOW INSP PRESSURE alarm indicating insufficient inspiratory pressure leading to inadequate ventilation or failure to reach set tidal volume.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient and provide alternate ventilation if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm LOW INSP PRESSURE alarm and reduced tidal volume.

steps.title: Check Patient Condition
steps.instructions: Assess airway patency and lung compliance.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure all tubing and connections are secure.

steps.title: Check for Leaks
steps.instructions: Inspect circuit, listen for leaks, verify cuff inflation.

steps.title: Verify Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm appropriate volume, pressure, and mode settings.

steps.title: Inspect Sensors
steps.instructions: Check flow sensor and remove condensation.

steps.title: Run EST
steps.instructions: Remove from service and run Extended Self-Test if needed.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming LOW INSP PRESSURE with reduced tidal volume.
CCR.Cause: Leak in patient circuit preventing adequate inspiratory pressure.
CCR.Resolution: Corrected circuit leak, verified normal pressure delivery, alarm cleared.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: INSPIRATION TOO LONG prolonged inspiratory time
description: Troubleshooting guide for the INSPIRATION TOO LONG alarm on the Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator, indicating prolonged inspiratory time causing improper breath cycling, potential air trapping, or unsafe ventilation timing.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Immediately assess the patient and be prepared to provide alternate ventilation if needed before continuing troubleshooting.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm and Behavior
steps.instructions: Confirm the ventilator is displaying INSPIRATION TOO LONG and observe whether inspiration is prolonged with delayed or absent cycling to exhalation.

steps.title: Check Patient Circuit for Obstruction or Flow Restriction
steps.instructions: Inspect the full patient circuit for kinks, water buildup, blocked filters, expiratory limb obstruction, or a saturated HME/filter.

steps.title: Check Patient Condition and Interaction with the Ventilator
steps.instructions: Assess for patient-ventilator asynchrony, breath stacking, or active inspiratory effort that may prolong inspiration.

steps.title: Verify Ventilator Settings
steps.instructions: Review inspiratory time, I:E ratio, and appropriateness of settings based on patient IBW.

steps.title: Check Flow Sensors and Circuit Components
steps.instructions: Inspect the flow sensor for contamination, moisture, proper placement, and secure external connections.

steps.title: Run Ventilator Self Tests
steps.instructions: Run the SST and EST and verify both tests complete without errors.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: If the alarm clears, verify normal ventilation waveform, confirm the alarm does not return, and then return the unit to service.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming 'INSPIRATION TOO LONG' during use with prolonged inspiratory phase observed.
CCR.Cause: Restricted circuit airflow and/or inspiratory time setting exceeded expected limits, causing delayed cycling to exhalation.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared circuit obstruction, verified settings, cleaned and reseated flow sensor, and ran SST/EST. Alarm cleared and unit returned to service.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: VOLUME NOT DELIVERED Pressure exceeds allowable limits preventing delivery
description: Alarm indicating failure to deliver target tidal volume due to high airway pressure limits or resistance.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient immediately and provide alternate ventilation if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm alarm presence and observe pressure and volume behavior.

steps.title: Check Patient Condition
steps.instructions: Assess airway patency, compliance, and obstruction causes.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit
steps.instructions: Check for kinks, water buildup, or blocked components.

steps.title: Verify Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure all circuit connections are secure and intact.

steps.title: Check Pressure Limit
steps.instructions: Evaluate 2PPEAK setting and adjust if too restrictive.

steps.title: Review Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm ventilator settings match patient requirements.

steps.title: Drain and Replace
steps.instructions: Remove condensation and replace clogged filters.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming VOLUME NOT DELIVERED with low tidal volume observed.
CCR.Cause: High airway pressure due to obstruction, restrictive setting, or internal fault.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared obstruction and adjusted settings or removed device from service if unresolved.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: NO AIR SUPPLY Loss of air source or compressor failure
description: NO AIR SUPPLY alarm indicating loss of compressed air, preventing proper ventilation and breath delivery.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess the patient immediately and provide alternate ventilation if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm NO AIR SUPPLY alarm and observe loss of airflow or pressure.

steps.title: Check Air Supply Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure hose is connected and wall outlet is active with correct gas source.

steps.title: Inspect Air Hose
steps.instructions: Check for kinks, damage, or disconnections and replace if needed.

steps.title: Check Air Inlet Filter
steps.instructions: Inspect and clean or replace if blocked.

steps.title: Verify Compressor Function
steps.instructions: Listen for compressor operation if equipped.

steps.title: Check Pressure Connections
steps.instructions: Ensure external pressure sensing connections are secure.

steps.title: Run EST
steps.instructions: Perform Extended Self Test and observe for failures.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming NO AIR SUPPLY, unable to deliver breaths.
CCR.Cause: Disconnected air hose or failed internal air supply component.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected air supply or removed device from service for repair.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: NO O2 SUPPLY Loss of oxygen source
description: Alarm indicating loss of oxygen input, preventing delivery of oxygen-enriched ventilation to the patient.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient immediately and provide alternate ventilation if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm NO O2 SUPPLY alarm and observe FiO2 drop and alarm behavior.

steps.title: Check Oxygen Connection
steps.instructions: Ensure hose is securely connected at wall and ventilator.

steps.title: Verify Wall Supply
steps.instructions: Test wall oxygen outlet with known-good device.

steps.title: Inspect Hose
steps.instructions: Check for kinks, cracks, or damage and replace if needed.

steps.title: Check Inlet Filter
steps.instructions: Inspect and replace oxygen inlet filter if clogged.

steps.title: Check Pressure Connections
steps.instructions: Verify external pressure sensing connections are secure.

steps.title: Run EST
steps.instructions: Perform extended self-test to confirm system integrity.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming NO O2 SUPPLY and unable to deliver oxygen.
CCR.Cause: Disconnected oxygen hose or failed wall oxygen supply.
CCR.Resolution: Reconnected oxygen source and verified proper oxygen delivery. Alarm cleared.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: O2 SENSOR Oxygen sensor out of calibration or failed
description: O2 SENSOR alarm indicating inaccurate oxygen readings or failure of the oxygen sensor, impacting FiO2 monitoring and safe ventilation delivery.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient stability, confirm ventilation, and use alternate monitoring or backup ventilation if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm O2 SENSOR alarm and observe inaccurate or missing FiO2 readings.

steps.title: Calibrate Sensor
steps.instructions: Perform oxygen sensor calibration using proper gas source and follow device prompts.

steps.title: Check Oxygen Supply
steps.instructions: Verify stable oxygen source, proper hose connections, and adequate pressure.

steps.title: Inspect Sensor
steps.instructions: Check sensor seating, cleanliness, and condition. Reseat or replace if needed.

steps.title: Replace Sensor
steps.instructions: Install a known-good sensor if calibration fails and repeat calibration process.

steps.title: Disable Sensor if Needed
steps.instructions: Disable sensor per protocol if clinically appropriate and use alternative monitoring.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator displaying O2 SENSOR alarm with inaccurate FiO2 readings.
CCR.Cause: Oxygen sensor failed calibration and showed unstable readings due to degradation.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced oxygen sensor, calibrated successfully, and verified proper FiO2 readings.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: ↑O2% Measured oxygen concentration above set value
description: Alarm indicating measured oxygen concentration exceeds the set FiO₂, risking hyperoxia and inaccurate ventilation delivery.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient oxygenation, reduce FiO₂ if needed, and prepare backup ventilation.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm ↑O2% alarm and compare measured vs set FiO₂.

steps.title: Check Oxygen Supply
steps.instructions: Verify correct gas connections and proper supply pressures.

steps.title: Calibrate Oxygen Sensor
steps.instructions: Perform calibration using stable gas sources and follow prompts.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit and Environment
steps.instructions: Check for leaks, improper setup, or external oxygen influence.

steps.title: Verify Settings
steps.instructions: Ensure FiO₂ settings match intended therapy.

steps.title: Escalate if Needed
steps.instructions: Remove from service if alarm persists after external checks.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming ↑O2% with measured FiO₂ higher than set value.
CCR.Cause: Oxygen sensor drift or gas control issue causing inaccurate measurement.
CCR.Resolution: Attempted calibration; removed device from service when alarm persisted.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: ↓O2% Measured O2 below set value
description: Alarm indicating delivered oxygen concentration is below the set value, risking inadequate patient oxygenation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient oxygenation immediately and provide supplemental or backup ventilation if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm ↓O2% alarm and observe delivered FiO2 compared to set value.

steps.title: Check Oxygen Supply
steps.instructions: Verify hose connection, wall source function, and absence of leaks or kinks.

steps.title: Inspect Inlet Components
steps.instructions: Check oxygen inlet filter and fittings for restriction or leaks.

steps.title: Calibrate O2 Sensor
steps.instructions: Perform sensor calibration using appropriate gas and stable conditions.

steps.title: Run EST
steps.instructions: Execute Extended Self-Test to identify oxygen-related faults.

steps.title: Check PSOL
steps.instructions: Verify oxygen valve function and FiO2 response to adjustments.

steps.title: Replace Sensor
steps.instructions: Replace oxygen sensor if calibration fails or readings remain inaccurate.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming ↓O2% with delivered FiO2 lower than set value.
CCR.Cause: Oxygen sensor failed calibration and produced inaccurate readings.
CCR.Resolution: Replaced oxygen sensor, recalibrated system, and confirmed accurate FiO2 delivery.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: ↑VTE Exhaled tidal volume above limit
description: Alarm indicating exhaled tidal volume exceeds set limits, risking over-ventilation and potential lung injury.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient for over-ventilation and prepare backup ventilation if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm ↑VTE alarm and observe elevated exhaled tidal volume and waveform behavior.

steps.title: Check Ventilator Settings
steps.instructions: Verify tidal volume, alarm limits, and ventilation mode are appropriate.

steps.title: Assess Patient Condition
steps.instructions: Evaluate for increased compliance or strong spontaneous breathing efforts.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit and Sensors
steps.instructions: Check flow sensor, tubing, and connections for issues affecting measurement.

steps.title: Run Self Tests
steps.instructions: Perform SST and EST to confirm ventilator system functionality.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming ↑VTE with higher-than-set exhaled tidal volumes during operation.
CCR.Cause: Ventilator settings and circuit verified; SST/EST performed. Persistent alarm suggests internal sensing or control issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ventilator from service, labeled out of service, and sent for repair evaluation.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: ↓VTE MAND Mandatory tidal volume below limit
description: Alarm indicating mandatory tidal volume is below the set limit, risking inadequate ventilation and hypoventilation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient ventilation immediately and provide manual or backup ventilation if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm ↓VTE MAND alarm and observe low delivered tidal volume during mandatory breaths.

steps.title: Check Patient Condition
steps.instructions: Assess airway patency, secretions, tube kinks, and lung compliance issues.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit
steps.instructions: Check for leaks, loose connections, or disconnections in the breathing circuit.

steps.title: Check Ventilator Settings
steps.instructions: Verify tidal volume, pressure limits, and mode configuration.

steps.title: Run SST
steps.instructions: Perform Short Self Test and review for failures.

steps.title: Stop When Resolved
steps.instructions: Return to service once normal tidal volume is restored and stable.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming ↓VTE MAND with low delivered tidal volume during mandatory breaths.
CCR.Cause: Unable to restore proper tidal volume after verifying patient airway, circuit integrity, and ventilator settings; SST performed—suspected internal ventilator issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ventilator from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for repair or evaluation.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: ↓VTE SPONT Spontaneous tidal volume below limit
description: Alarm indicating low spontaneous tidal volume, potentially leading to inadequate patient ventilation.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient respiratory status and provide manual or backup ventilation if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm ↓VTE SPONT alarm and observe low spontaneous tidal volumes during breathing.

steps.title: Check Patient Condition
steps.instructions: Evaluate respiratory effort, sedation, fatigue, and airway patency.

steps.title: Check Ventilator Settings
steps.instructions: Verify mode, pressure support, and alarm limits are appropriate.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit
steps.instructions: Check for leaks, disconnections, kinks, or obstructions.

steps.title: Run SST
steps.instructions: Perform Short Self Test to verify ventilator function.

steps.title: Isolate Cause
steps.instructions: Determine if issue is patient, settings, circuit, or device related.

steps.title: Resolve and Confirm
steps.instructions: Ensure alarm clears and tidal volumes return to expected range.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming ↓VTE SPONT with low spontaneous tidal volumes.
CCR.Cause: Low spontaneous tidal volume due to patient fatigue, improper settings, or circuit leak.
CCR.Resolution: Adjusted settings, checked circuit, performed SST, and removed from service if unresolved.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: ↑VE TOT Minute volume above limit
description: Alarm indicating total minute ventilation exceeds set limits, risking over-ventilation and patient instability.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient status and provide backup ventilation if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm ↑VE TOT alarm and elevated minute volume readings.

steps.title: Check Patient Condition
steps.instructions: Evaluate for increased respiratory drive due to clinical causes.

steps.title: Check Settings
steps.instructions: Verify VT, rate, support modes, and alarm limits.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit and Sensors
steps.instructions: Check flow sensor, tubing, and expiratory valve.

steps.title: Run SST
steps.instructions: Perform system self test.

steps.title: Run EST
steps.instructions: Perform extended diagnostics if needed.

steps.title: Resolve
steps.instructions: Confirm alarm clears and values normalize.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming ↑VE TOT with high minute ventilation readings.
CCR.Cause: Minute volume exceeded limits due to patient condition or possible sensor issue.
CCR.Resolution: Verified settings, checked circuit, ran diagnostics, removed from service if unresolved.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: ↓VE TOT Minute volume below limit
description: Alarm indicating total minute ventilation is below the set limit, risking inadequate ventilation and CO2 retention.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient and provide manual ventilation or switch to backup device if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm ↓VE TOT alarm and observe low minute volume readings during operation.

steps.title: Check Patient Condition
steps.instructions: Assess airway patency, respiratory effort, and possible apnea or sedation effects.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit
steps.instructions: Check for leaks, loose connections, or disconnections in the breathing circuit.

steps.title: Check Settings
steps.instructions: Verify tidal volume, respiratory rate, and alarm limits are appropriate.

steps.title: Evaluate Sensors
steps.instructions: Inspect flow sensor for contamination or moisture and confirm stable readings.

steps.title: Run SST
steps.instructions: Perform Short Self Test and confirm it passes without errors.

steps.title: Isolate Cause
steps.instructions: Determine if issue is patient-related, circuit-related, configuration-related, or device-related.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming ↓VE TOT with low measured minute volume during patient use.
CCR.Cause: Leak identified in patient circuit causing reduced delivered and measured ventilation volume.
CCR.Resolution: Re-secured circuit connections, verified no leaks, confirmed normal VE readings. Alarm cleared and ventilator returned to service.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: ↑PPEAK Peak airway pressure exceeds limit
description: Alarm indicating high airway pressure which may cause barotrauma, inadequate ventilation, or breath termination.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient immediately and provide manual or backup ventilation if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm ↑PPEAK alarm and observe elevated airway pressure readings during inspiration.

steps.title: Check Patient Condition
steps.instructions: Evaluate for coughing, bronchospasm, or secretions increasing airway resistance.

steps.title: Inspect ET Tube
steps.instructions: Check for kinks, biting, misplacement, or obstruction; suction if needed.

steps.title: Check Circuit
steps.instructions: Inspect for kinks, water buildup, or blocked filters in the breathing circuit.

steps.title: Review Settings
steps.instructions: Verify tidal volume, inspiratory flow, and pressure limits are appropriate.

steps.title: Run Self-Test
steps.instructions: Perform SST/EST to rule out internal faults if no external cause is found.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming ↑PPEAK with high airway pressures and breath termination.
CCR.Cause: Kinked circuit tubing increasing resistance and airway pressure.
CCR.Resolution: Corrected tubing issue, verified normal pressures, alarm cleared, returned to service.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: ↑PMEAN Mean airway pressure exceeds limit
description: Troubleshooting guide for ↑PMEAN alarm indicating elevated mean airway pressure and risk of lung overdistension or ventilation issues

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient and provide manual or backup ventilation if needed

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm ↑PMEAN alarm and elevated pressure readings

steps.title: Check Patient Condition
steps.instructions: Evaluate compliance, airway patency, and patient interaction

steps.title: Inspect Circuit
steps.instructions: Check for kinks, water, or blocked filters

steps.title: Verify Settings
steps.instructions: Review PEEP, inspiratory time, and tidal volume

steps.title: Check Expiratory Flow
steps.instructions: Ensure proper exhalation and no obstruction

steps.title: Run Self Tests
steps.instructions: Perform SST and EST to identify internal faults

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming ↑PMEAN with elevated airway pressures during use.
CCR.Cause: No external obstruction or setting issue identified; suspected internal pressure regulation issue.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ventilator from service, labeled Out of Service, and sent for evaluation.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: ↑PVENT Inspiratory pressure exceeds 100 cmH₂O
description: High inspiratory pressure alarm indicating pressure exceeds 100 cmH₂O, risking lung injury and improper ventilation delivery.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient immediately and provide manual or backup ventilation if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm ↑PVENT alarm and high inspiratory pressure readings.

steps.title: Check Patient Condition
steps.instructions: Inspect airway for obstruction, bronchospasm, or ET tube issues.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit
steps.instructions: Check for kinks, condensation, and blockages in circuit components.

steps.title: Review Settings
steps.instructions: Verify tidal volume, pressure limits, and PEEP settings.

steps.title: Run SST
steps.instructions: Perform System Self Test to evaluate device function.

steps.title: Run EST
steps.instructions: Perform Extended Self Test if issue persists.

steps.title: Swap Components
steps.instructions: Replace circuit and filters to rule out external causes.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming ↑PVENT with high inspiratory pressure and low volume delivery.
CCR.Cause: Obstruction in breathing circuit or patient airway causing elevated pressure.
CCR.Resolution: Cleared obstruction or replaced circuit; performed SST and verified normal operation.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: ↑fTOT Respiratory rate exceeds set limit
description: Guide for troubleshooting ↑fTOT alarm indicating respiratory rate exceeds limit, including patient assessment, trigger sensitivity, and circuit checks.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient and provide manual or backup ventilation if needed.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm ↑fTOT alarm and elevated respiratory rate readings.

steps.title: Check Patient Condition
steps.instructions: Evaluate for distress, hypoxia, pain, or metabolic demand.

steps.title: Check Trigger Sensitivity
steps.instructions: Adjust sensitivity and assess for auto-triggering.

steps.title: Inspect Circuit
steps.instructions: Check for leaks, loose connections, and cuff inflation.

steps.title: Review Settings
steps.instructions: Confirm alarm limits and ventilator mode are appropriate.

steps.title: Run SST
steps.instructions: Perform system self test.

steps.title: Run EST
steps.instructions: Perform extended self test if needed.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator alarming ↑fTOT with elevated respiratory rate.
CCR.Cause: Patient tachypnea or auto-triggering due to circuit leak or sensitivity settings.
CCR.Resolution: Adjusted settings, corrected circuit issues, tested ventilator, removed from service if unresolved.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: SCREEN BLOCK Blocked beam or touchscreen fault
description: SCREEN BLOCK alarm causing touchscreen unresponsiveness due to obstruction or interface failure, limiting ventilator control.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Confirm patient ventilation is stable and prepare backup ventilation if control is limited.

steps.title: Verify the Reported Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm SCREEN BLOCK alarm is displayed and touchscreen is unresponsive or intermittent.

steps.title: Check for Physical Obstruction
steps.instructions: Remove any objects touching the screen such as tubing, drapes, or debris.

steps.title: Clean the Touchscreen
steps.instructions: Wipe with approved cleaner, dry thoroughly, and remove residue or moisture.

steps.title: Check Environmental Factors
steps.instructions: Ensure no moisture, condensation, or cables are interfering with the screen.

steps.title: Perform System Reset
steps.instructions: Power down, disconnect AC power briefly, and restart the ventilator.

steps.title: Evaluate Touchscreen Function
steps.instructions: Test responsiveness across the screen and check for dead zones or continuous input.

CCR.Complaint: Unit displaying SCREEN BLOCK alarm with unresponsive touchscreen.
CCR.Cause: No obstruction found; touchscreen remained unresponsive after cleaning and reset.
CCR.Resolution: Removed ventilator from service and sent for repair evaluation.

title: Puritan Bennett 840 Ventilator - Alarm: PROCEDURE ERROR Patient connected before setup complete
description: PROCEDURE ERROR alarm indicating setup interruption due to patient connection before completion, preventing safe ventilator initialization.

steps.title: Ensure Patient Safety First
steps.instructions: Assess patient ventilation immediately and provide manual or backup ventilation if setup is incomplete.

steps.title: Verify Alarm
steps.instructions: Confirm PROCEDURE ERROR is displayed during startup or setup and that normal operation is not available.

steps.title: Check Setup Sequence
steps.instructions: Determine if patient was connected before completing setup or SST.

steps.title: Complete Setup Properly
steps.instructions: Disconnect patient, complete full setup and SST, and follow all prompts.

steps.title: Reconnect and Test
steps.instructions: Reconnect patient and verify normal operation without alarms.

steps.title: Escalate if Needed
steps.instructions: Power cycle, retry setup, or remove from service if alarm persists.

CCR.Complaint: Ventilator displayed PROCEDURE ERROR alarm during setup and would not initiate ventilation.
CCR.Cause: Patient connected prior to completion of ventilator setup and SST process.
CCR.Resolution: Disconnected patient, completed full setup and SST, reconnected patient, verified normal operation.
